description: Document and codify the product rule that any recurring invoice window containing matching billable time with `approval_status !== 'APPROVED'` is blocked in full, including windows that also contain non-time charges.
Implement contract-hourly approval-blocker detection using the same effective service-period, service-matching, assignment, client, and work-item semantics as approved recurring billing selection.
Implement unresolved/non-contract approval-blocker detection only when unresolved/non-contract time is part of the recurring selection scope being represented.
Treat any non-approved status as blocking while ignoring already invoiced time entries in approval-blocker counts.
Extend recurring due-work row and/or candidate types with approval-blocker metadata such as blocked entry count and approval-specific blocked reason.
Populate approval-blocker metadata during recurring due-work shaping so blocked windows can be classified before the UI renders Automatic Invoices.
Mark recurring due-work candidates with approval blockers as `canGenerate = false` and attach approval-specific blocked messaging instead of a generic ineligible state.
Add a dedicated Needs Approval section above Ready to Invoice in Automatic Invoices.
Move approval-blocked grouped windows out of Ready to Invoice and into Needs Approval while preserving existing grouped presentation semantics.
Render each Needs Approval grouped row with client, service period, invoice window, and `X unapproved entries`.
Add a `Review Approvals` action on Needs Approval rows that routes operators to the existing approval workflow.
Ensure Needs Approval rows are non-selectable, non-previewable for generation, and non-generatable from Automatic Invoices.
Preserve existing Ready to Invoice selection, preview, and generation behavior for windows that do not have approval blockers.
Centralize recurring approval-blocker detection in a shared helper or service so UI classification and server generation enforcement use the same logic.
Re-check approval blockers immediately before recurring invoice creation and reject blocked windows server-side with a descriptive error that includes the blocked entry count.
Ensure grouped recurring billing runs continue processing unrelated eligible windows even when one selected target is rejected for approval blockers.
Block mixed-charge invoice windows in full when matching billable time is not approved, rather than allowing partial recurring invoices for fixed or other non-time charges.
Make approval-blocked windows transition cleanly from Needs Approval to Ready to Invoice after the relevant entries are approved and the page is refreshed.
Update in-product explanatory copy and plan/runbook documentation to state that the entire invoice window is blocked until the billable time is approved.
